title: corydalis fabacea proles pumila
intro: Corydalis fabacea proles pumila, also known as Corydalis pumila, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family Papaveraceae. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant contains alkaloids such as tetrahydroprotoberberine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, and has been used to treat a range of health issues including pain, anxiety, and insomnia. Corydalis pumila can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality.
